title: ononis natrix var. reesei
intro: Ononis natrix var. reesei, also known as the snake's eye or stinking goosefoot, is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves with distinctive red or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Ononis natrix var. reesei include reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various health concerns. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in poultices and creams to treat skin conditions.
